preamble: The shift from the velar nasal [ŋ] to the voiceless postalveolar fricative [ʃ] (sh) is a multi-stage process typically driven by palatalization. It involves the fronting of the nasal to a palatal position [ɲ] followed by de-nasalization and fricativization, usually triggered by following high front vowels.

examples.note: Historical fronting and fricativization of velar initials before high front vowels in various Chinese dialectal developments.

sources: Handel, Z. (2009). Old Chinese and Forestalling the 'Velar Palatalization' Problem.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
